definition: I2S synthesizes a structural count or length field (ELF section count, font record count, loop-entry integer) so the loop guard evaluates true and the loop body executes, reaching branches gated behind a positive-count check.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must parse as an ELF whose header fields (magic 7f 45 4c 46, class/endianness/version bytes at 0x04-0x07, and the section-header-count field) decode to a section_count() value of 2 or more, so the loop guard i < elf.section_count() is true on the first iteration (i=1).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seeds carry well-formed ELF identification bytes (e.g. 7f 45 4c 46 02 02 03 00 ... and 7f 45 4c 46 02 01 ...) plus header fields that make section_count() large: in the W role section_count() is invoked 286000 times and the loop guard at line 856 takes the true side (T=57200), whereas the loser does not, so the loop body and ReadSection run only for the winner.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds keep section_count() effectively at <=1 so the line-856 guard never takes the true branch (L role at line 856 shows T=0 F=29); section_count() fires only 222 times in L vs 286000 in W, and ReadSection (line 536) fires 0 times in L, meaning the loop body is never entered.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand into input offsets matching the other operand, letting it plant the exact header byte values (magic/class/section-count) that the ELF parser compares against, so the decoded section_count() crosses the loop bound. Naive has only blind havoc with no CMP introspection and cannot reliably hit the multi-byte header constraints.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer from sfnt-magic and FOURCC table-tag comparisons and substitutes the expected operands (sfnt version 00 01 00 00 and tag literals like GSUB/GPOS/MATH) at the matching input offsets, making array length >= hb_min_size so the false branch is taken.
signature.one_line: I2S plants sfnt magic and FOURCC tags to pass array length >= hb_min_size

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be a well-formed OpenType font file: a valid sfnt header plus recognized 4-byte table tags so the array length reaches hb_min_size(OpenTypeFontFile).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An OpenType sfnt blob whose leading bytes match the expected sfnt-version magic and whose table-directory entries carry recognized 4-byte FOURCC tags (GSUB/GPOS/MATH/...), so the parsed array length is >= hb_min_size(OpenTypeFontFile) and the gate at hb-array.hh:252 takes its false branch (returning a real reinterpret_cast<const T*> rather than &Null(T)).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winning seed begins with the sfnt version 00 01 00 00 and embeds literal OpenType table tags at offset 0x0c (GSUB, GPOS, MATH, GPO), giving the array enough length to clear hb_min_size and resolve the false branch; the branch is hit 12529 (cmplog) / 5642 (vpc) times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short and lack the sfnt magic and table-tag structure (e.g. a 6-byte seed 87 0e 0d 0e 22 0e), so length stays below hb_min_size and only the true (Null) side is ever taken; loser hitcount on the branch is 0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the program's expected operands (the sfnt magic and FOURCC tag literals) directly into the input at the matching offsets, manufacturing the exact byte sequences the length/tag gate requires; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit those multi-byte literals by chance, which they never do in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a length-vs-min-size gate that returns either a Null pointer or a real cast pointer.
The globally blocked side is the false branch, i.e. the side reached only when length is large enough.

Winning seeds embed literal OpenType table tags such as GPOS at offset 0x0c.
Offset 0x0c is concentrated on tag-letter bytes in W (G dominant) but not in L.
The winner resolves the branch in all 10 trials with high hitcount while the loser stays blocked at hitcount 0.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace copies sfnt magic (00 01 00 00) and 4-byte table-tag FOURCCs ('cmap','GSUN') from intercepted CMP operands directly into input, building the multi-page bit-set structure value_profile cannot assemble.
signature.one_line: I2S plants sfnt magic and table-tag FourCCs enabling multi-page bit-set

analysis.summary_one_line: A multi-table sfnt font (>=2 page-map entries so pages.length!=0) must drive resize past the small-set fast path to reach the false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid sfnt/font header whose parsed table directory yields a multi-page bit-set: pages.length must be non-zero (or count!=1) so that the small-set guard at line 92 fails and the false branch is taken. This requires byte-exact sfnt magic (00 01 00 00), a plausible numTables/offset record, and recognizable table tags (cmap, GSUN/GSUB-like) at fixed offsets that survive harfbuzz's structural sanity checks deep enough to build a second bit-set page.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds begin with the canonical TrueType header 00 01 00 00 followed by a table-directory region containing ASCII tags 'cmap', 'GSUN' and dense non-zero structural bytes at fixed offsets (e.g. 0x06 ff, 0x07 80, 0x17 21, 0x1b-0x1e 'GSUN'). These pinned multi-byte literals let parsing proceed far enough that the bit-set acquires more than one page, so resize is called with pages.length!=0 and the false branch (resolved 17134 hits) is taken.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds are short (13-57 bytes) and lack the byte-exact header/tag structure: offset 0x00 holds e0/fe/92 rather than 00, and offsets 0x05-0x20 hold scattered 00/garbage instead of the concentrated 'cmap'/'GSUN' tag bytes. These inputs never build a second page, so resize sees pages.length==0 && count==1, takes the true branch, and the false side stays blocked (loser hitcount 0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which copies the operands of intercepted comparisons (the sfnt magic compare, the 4-byte table-tag fourcc compares) directly into the input at the offset the operand was loaded from, byte-exactly placing 00 01 00 00 / 'cmap' / 'GSUN'. value_profile has only the CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient and havoc/token mutators (no I2SRandReplace), so it cannot place the exact multi-byte tags and stalls before a second page is built.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of a small-set fast-path guard combining pages.length and count.
The globally blocked side is the false branch.
The only decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog beating value_profile with an I2S delta.
The winner resolves the false branch with high hitcount while the loser never hits it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=17134  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry the canonical sfnt header and recognizable table tags including cmap and GSUN.
Winner seeds contain a GSUN table tag in the directory region.
Loser-blocking seeds are short and lack the structured header bytes, starting with garbage like fe ff.
At least one loser-blocking seed is only 13 bytes long, far too short to build a multi-page table directory.
At the fixed sfnt magic/tag offsets the winner bytes are tightly concentrated while loser bytes are scattered, a dataflow signal of pinned literals.
Winner pins the GSUN tag bytes at offsets 0x1b-0x1e while the loser does not.
value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator on top of havoc/token.
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace entirely.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where value_profile reaches the false branch as often as value_profile_cmplog when the small-set guard is gated only by a Hamming-near table tag (i.e. CMP_MAP gradient alone solves it without needing byte-exact I2S substitution).

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from byte-exact tag placement to pages.length becoming non-zero is inferred from source plus seed structure; the prompt does not directly show the parse step that creates the second bit-set page.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes intercepted sfnt version word and 4-byte table-tag literals ('cmap','GSUN') into input, populating multi-major page_map that value_profile cannot build without exact tag bytes.
signature.one_line: I2S plants sfnt version and table tags to populate bfind-searched page_map

analysis.summary_one_line: Input needs a valid sfnt font header with multiple distinct major-codepoint table tags so bfind probes a populated page_map.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed OpenType/sfnt container: 4-byte sfnt version 00 01 00 00 at offset 0, a non-trivial numTables, and recognizable 4-byte table tags (e.g. 'cmap', 'GSU.', tag bytes at 0x1b-0x1e = 53 55 4e = 'SUN') that drive glyph-set population across several distinct majors, so that page_map is non-empty and bfind (the binary-search lookup at line 912) returns false (no match yet) on a fresh major, forcing the insert/store-closest path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries the constant prefix 00 01 00 00 00 07 ff 80 ... 63 6d 61 70 ('cmap') and 47 53 55 4e ('GSUN') tags; these are font tables that get parsed and add glyph ranges across many majors, so page_for is invoked 3520 times (W) and page_map_t::cmp runs 18400 times (W) — a populated, repeatedly-searched page_map.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (13-57 bytes) and lack the sfnt prefix and table-tag structure; page_for fires only 20 times (L) and page_map_t::cmp 0 times (L), so the bit-set is essentially empty/never bfind-searched and the false (store-closest) side at line 912 is never taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which copies intercepted comparison operands (sfnt version word, the 4-byte table tags compared during table-directory parsing) directly into the input at the offsets being compared. This lets vpc reconstruct the exact magic/tag bytes that build a multi-major page_map; plain value_profile has only the CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient and no operand-substitution mutator, so it cannot place the exact tag bytes and stays blocked.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of a binary-search lookup with store-closest into page_map.
The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog > value_profile with delta I2S.
Winner resolves 9/10 while loser blocks 10/10, with the branch hit 2860 times by the winner and 0 by the loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=2860  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds carry a constant sfnt/font-table prefix including the 'cmap' tag.
Winner seeds also contain a 'GSUN' table tag region.
Loser-blocking seeds are short and unstructured, e.g. 13 bytes with no font header.
page_for and page_map_t::cmp run thousands of times for the winner but never (cmp) or only 20 times (page_for) for the loser.
The bfind branch at line 912 is exercised on both sides for the winner but L only reaches the true side, never the false (store-closest) side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 912  T=3070 F=375  T=20 F=0  if (!page_map.bfind (map, &i, HB_NOT_FOUND_STORE_CLOSEST))  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog has I2SRandReplace in its mutator stack.
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, relying only on the CMP_MAP distance bucket feedback.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A value_profile (no-I2S) trial that resolves the false side of line 912 at comparable rate to vpc, or vpc resolving it on inputs that contain no recognizable sfnt version word / table tags (i.e. with the tag-substitution mutator disabled).

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact comparison whose operands I2S substitutes (sfnt version word vs each 4-byte table tag) is inferred from seed structure, not directly traced to a specific CMP callsite in the prompt's source window.

members.function: bool OT::UnsizedArrayOf<AAT::FeatureName>::sanitize<AAT::feat const*>(hb_sanitize_context_t*, unsigned int, AAT::feat const*&&) const
members.source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < count; i++)

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace buffers tag/count CMP operands per execution and substitutes the AAT table-tag literal and count value into the input, building a valid sanitize-passing table with count>0 that value_profile's Hamming gradient cannot reliably land.
signature.one_line: I2S plants sfnt magic and AAT table tag so count>0 loop-entry branch fires

analysis.summary_one_line: Input needs a valid sfnt header with a non-zero table count and a recognized 4-byte table tag so the array sanitize loop has elements to iterate.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed OpenType header: magic 00 01 00 00 at offset 0, a non-zero numTables/count field (offsets 0x04-0x05), and one of the literal 4-byte sfnt/AAT table tags (e.g. 'feat', 'trak', 'name', 'VORG') at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, so sanitize_shallow's check_array succeeds and count>0 makes the for-loop enter its body (true side).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the 00 01 00 00 sfnt magic, a non-zero count byte at offset 0x05, and ASCII table tags at 0x0c-0x0f ('feat','trak','name'), the exact bytes whose CMP against harfbuzz's known-tag constants the I2S stage can satisfy by substitution.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 00 in offsets 0x05-0x10 (count and tag region all zero), so the tag never matches a recognized table and count stays 0, taking the false side and never entering the loop body.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace: cmplog buffers the failing tag/count CMP operands per execution and substitutes the harfbuzz-side comparison constant (the table tag, the count check) directly into the input, planting the literal bytes that plain value_profile's Hamming-distance feedback cannot reliably assemble at these multi-byte tag offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the loop-entry test of an array sanitize over font table records; the true side is globally blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:     for (unsigned int i = 0; i < count; i++)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Entering the loop body requires sanitize_shallow to succeed via len.sanitize and check_array; the body is only hit by the winner.
The 1-hop caller gates the loop on check_array(arrayZ, len), reached only by the winner.
Winner seeds carry the 00 01 00 00 sfnt magic and an ASCII table tag ('feat') at offsets 0x0c onward.
Another winner seed carries the 'trak' table tag at offsets 0x0c-0x0f.
Loser-blocking seeds are all-zero across the count and tag region (offsets 0x05-0x10), so no recognized tag and count=0.
The byte diff shows winner carries printable tag bytes at 0x0c-0x0f while loser carries 00 there.
At offset 0x05 the winner carries a non-zero count byte (mostly 01) while the loser carries 00.
value_profile_cmplog's mutator set adds I2SRandReplace on top of havoc/token, which value_profile lacks.
value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If value_profile resolves the branch at the same rate as value_profile_cmplog when the harness front-matter (sfnt magic + non-zero count + a recognized 4-byte table tag) is supplied pre-formed in the seed, showing the divergence is not driven by I2S supplying the multi-byte tag/count operands.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff is multi-tag and noisy (different winner seeds use feat/trak/name/VORG), so I cannot pin the divergence to a single fixed tag literal versus the more general 'any recognized tag + non-zero count' structural requirement.

members.function: OT::FeatureVariations::find_index(int const*, unsigned int, unsigned int*) const
members.source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < count; i++)

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts version and GPOS/GSUB tag integer comparisons and substitutes the captured operands at fixed input offsets, planting the exact magic/tag bytes that make varRecords.len > 0 so the FeatureVariations loop body is entered.
signature.one_line: I2S plants sfnt magic and GPOS/GSUB tag so FeatureVariations loop body fires

analysis.summary_one_line: Resolving the find_index loop requires a structurally-valid OpenType table header (00 01 00 00, GPOS/GSUB tag, valid offsets) so a varRecord exists to iterate.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed OpenType GSUB/GPOS layout table: the 4-byte version magic 00 01 00 00 at offset 0x00, a script/feature tag (GPOS/GSUB) at 0x0c-0x0f, and consistent table/offset fields (ff 1e 21, 00 18, etc.) so that sanitize passes and varRecords.len > 0, making the loop at L3281 enter its body (true branch).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal magic 00 01 00 00 at 0x00-0x03, the ASCII tags GPOS/GSUB at 0x0c-0x0f, and a stable structured tail (ff 1e 21 ... 00 18) at 0x10-0x17 across all 10 winner samples; the cov overlay shows the loop body L3283-L3289 hit only by W and ConditionSet::evaluate runs 315 times for W versus 0 for L.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are unstructured byte soup (e0 17 00 00..., fe ff ff ff..., repeated 3d/= runs) with no magic/tag at the fixed offsets, so sanitize rejects the table and varRecords.len stays 0; the loop never enters (T=0) and the false branch is taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, the only mutator value_profile lacks: cmplog intercepts the integer/tag comparisons (version equality, tag cmp) and substitutes the captured operand directly into the input at the comparison's byte offset, planting the exact magic/tag bytes that value_profile's Hamming-bucket feedback alone cannot land because the equality is all-or-nothing.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the loop-condition true branch of find_index iterating varRecords.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < count; i++)

evidence_trail.claim: The only decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog beating value_profile on the I2S delta.
Winner seeds carry the OpenType version magic and GPOS/GSUB tags at fixed offsets.
A winner seed shows the GSUB tag instead of GPOS, confirming the tag bytes are the discriminating structure.
The byte diff shows W is concentrated on the magic/tag bytes (00 01 00 00 at 0x00-0x03, GPOS/GSUB at 0x0c-0x0f) while L is dispersed.
Loser-blocking seeds are unstructured and lack the fixed-offset header.
ConditionSet::evaluate runs 315 times for the winner and 0 for the loser, showing only W enters the loop body and reaches the conditions.
The loop true branch is taken only by W (T=315) and never by L (T=0).
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace to the mutator stack that value_profile lacks.
value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, only havoc plus the CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient feedback.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where value_profile resolves the loop as often as value_profile_cmplog when the gate is an exact 4-byte equality on the OpenType magic/tag — showing CMP_MAP gradient alone suffices and I2S substitution is not the deciding factor.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff confirms W concentrates on the magic/tag offsets but does not directly prove cmplog substituted those exact bytes via I2S rather than havoc/token mutations landing them by luck; the single dash-row floor signal is a lower bound, not a precise count.

members.source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < this->lookup_count; i++)

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the FOURCC tag and sfnt-header integer comparisons and substitutes the expected operand bytes, bridging the multi-byte exact-match gate so that lookup_count becomes positive and the loop guard is true.
signature.one_line: I2S plants GPOS/GSUB FOURCC to make lookup_count positive for loop guard

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be a valid sfnt font carrying a parseable GPOS/GSUB table whose lookup_count is non-zero to enter the per-lookup loop.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed sfnt header (00 01 00 00 version, table directory) with a table record bearing the literal FOURCC tag 'GPOS' (or 'GSUB') at the tag offset, so that get_lookup_count() returns a value > 0 and the loop guard i < this->lookup_count is taken true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries the exact ASCII FOURCC 'GPOS'/'GSUB' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f and the canonical sfnt version prefix 00 01 00 00 at offsets 0x00-0x03, plus structured table-record bytes; the loop runs heavily (avg hitcount 77280 / 55420) and the per-lookup init at line 4457 is hit only in W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short or garbage (6, 13, 27, 35, 57 bytes) lacking the GPOS/GSUB FOURCC at 0x0c-0x0f; they never produce a parseable table directory, so lookup_count stays 0 and the loop guard is always false (blocked=10/10, hitcount 0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the FOURCC tag comparison and sfnt-header integer comparisons are logged, then substitutes the expected operand (the 'GPOS'/'GSUB' tag and header magic) into the input at the matching offsets — bridging the multi-byte exact-match gate that the plain havoc/token stack in naive/value_profile cannot brute-force.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the loop guard whose true side requires lookup_count > 0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < this->lookup_count; i++)

evidence_trail.claim: lookup_count is set from the parsed table just before the loop.
Winner seeds contain the literal FOURCC 'GPOS'/'GSUB' tag at offsets 0x0c-0x0f while loser seeds do not.
Winner seed bytes carry the sfnt version prefix and the GPOS FOURCC.
Loser-blocking seeds are tiny and unstructured, lacking any table directory.

evidence_trail.claim: The winner runs the loop with very high hit count while the loser never enters it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=77280  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The per-lookup init at line 4457 is exercised only in the winner role.
cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace which substitutes the matching CMP operand at the offsets where it occurs in the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ from their loser by exactly the I2S technique.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A naive or value_profile trial reaching the true branch (resolved>0) on an input lacking the GPOS/GSUB FOURCC, or a synthetic harness where removing the exact FOURCC tag-match gate leaves the cmplog>naive divergence intact.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff shows the FOURCC tag offset is data-dependent (a table directory can place the GPOS record at varying offsets), so the fixed 0x0c-0x0f location is seed-specific rather than a guaranteed structural offset.

members.source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < this->lookup_count; i++)

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts the 4-byte GPOS tag comparison via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp, logs the operand, and substitutes the literal into the input at the matching offset so lookup_count is non-zero and the destructor loop iterates.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes GPOS tag so destructor loop count exceeds zero

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a well-formed GPOS table (FOURCC 'GPOS' at offset 0x0c plus a valid header) so lookup_count > 0 drives the destructor loop true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte table tag 'GPOS' (47 50 4f 53) must appear at offset 0x0c so the sfnt table directory routes to GPOS parsing, and the GPOS header must parse with at least one lookup so accelerator_t::lookup_count is non-zero, making the destructor loop 'i < this->lookup_count' iterate (true branch).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winning-resolving seed places the literal FOURCC tag at offset 0x0c-0x0f: seeds 1/3/5 show 'GPOS' (47 50 4f 53) and seed 2 shows 'GSUB' (47 53 55 42); the BYTE DIFF confirms W is fully concentrated to 47('G') at 0x0c and 50('P')/53('S') at 0x0d-0x0f, indicating the tag is the dataflow-relevant operand the winner reproduces.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry no table tag at offset 0x0c (bytes there are 00/01/20/2c), so the sfnt directory never resolves a GPOS table, lookup_count stays 0, and the loop guard is false; their hitcount on the branch is 0 across all 10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the 4-byte tag comparison via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp, log the expected operand 'GPOS' into the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes that literal into the input at the matching offset; naive and value_profile have no I2SRandReplace mutator and cannot synthesize the exact FOURCC magic, so they stall.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the destructor loop guarded by this->lookup_count, true side blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < this->lookup_count; i++)

evidence_trail.claim: The true (loop-entering) side is the globally blocked side.
Winning seeds contain the literal 'GPOS' FOURCC tag at offset 0x0c.
Another winning seed carries the related 'GSUB' table tag at offset 0x0c.
At offset 0x0c the winner bytes are fully concentrated on 47('G') while the loser bytes diverge to 00/01/20/2c.
At offset 0x0d the winner is concentrated on 50('P')/53('S'), the tag's second byte.
The winner resolves the branch in all 10 trials while the loser is blocked in all 10 with zero hitcount.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=77280  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching offsets in the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, so it cannot perform the operand substitution that the vpc winner uses.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a 4-byte magic-tag equality where cmplog/vpc fail to resolve the true side while naive/value_profile resolve it, showing I2S substitution is not what drives the divergence.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The two dash rows sit at depths 29-30, far back in a 31-level chain dominated by havoc ops, so the I2S contribution to this specific leaf is an inference rather than a directly-tagged step.

members.source_line: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < num_features; i++) {

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts the 4-byte tag comparison inside get_feature_tag and substitutes the GSUB/GPOS magic FOURCC directly into the input, making get_feature_count positive so the for-loop true branch fires.
signature.one_line: I2S plants GSUB/GPOS tag to make feature count positive for loop entry

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a 4-byte FOURCC table tag (e.g. GSUB/GPOS) so the LangSys feature list is non-empty and the loop iterates.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed OpenType sfnt header whose table directory contains a 4-byte FOURCC tag matching a layout table the harness looks up (GSUB / GPOS), so get_gsubgpos_table resolves a real table, get_lang_sys yields a LangSys with num_features > 0, and the for-loop at line 1001 takes its true branch.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed embeds an exact ASCII FOURCC table tag at the table-directory offset: seeds 1-2 and 5 contain 'GSUB', seeds 3-4 contain 'GPOS', sitting at offset 0x0c onward where the W byte set is concentrated on 47(G)/50(P)/53(S)/4f(O)/55(U)/42(B). These literal 4-byte tags drive get_feature_count() positive and the loop iterates 76930 (cmplog) / 11528 (vpc) times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry no GSUB/GPOS FOURCC at those offsets — at 0x0c-0x0f the L byte sets are scattered (00, 20, 18, 6a, 6f, fa, etc.) with no concentrated tag literal, so get_gsubgpos_table returns the empty default, num_features is 0, the loop body never runs (hitcount 0), and line 1001 stays on its false side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the 4-byte tag comparison inside get_feature_tag / table-tag lookup and record (input-operand, expected-tag) into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then substitutes the magic FOURCC ('GSUB'/'GPOS') directly into the input at the matched offset, synthesizing the exact-literal tag that random havoc byte mutation in naive/value_profile is astronomically unlikely to land on a 4-byte literal. Both decisive pairs differ from their loser by exactly the I2SRandReplace stage.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ by the I2S technique and both winners fully resolve while losers fully block.
The second decisive pair isolates the same I2S technique delta (vpc over vp).
The blocker is the feature-iteration loop, and its body is reached only on the winner role, gated by a feature_tag equality compare.
The loop body / feature-index read is hit only by winner-resolving seeds.
Winner seeds embed the literal ASCII table tag GSUB at the table-directory offset.
Other winner seeds carry GPOS instead of GSUB, both valid layout-table FOURCC tags.
At the tag offsets the winner byte set concentrates on the FOURCC letters while the loser set is scattered with no tag literal.
On the loser role the blocker loop never enters its body (true side count zero), confirming an empty feature list.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff aligns the FOURCC at offset 0x0c but does not prove the exact compare I2S solves is the table-tag lookup versus the inner feature_tag==get_feature_tag compare; either way the operand is a 4-byte tag literal.

hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted tag comparison (the HB_TAG sfnt-table-tag compare) from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the 'GPOS' operand into the input at the matching offset, planting the exact 4-byte tag that naive/value_profile must find by blind havoc.
